title: Bitter Light Mexican Paneer Stew
description: A gentle yet slightly bitter one‑pot stew that fuses Mexican norteño flavors with creamy paneer, perfect for a light gluten‑free dinner.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and jalapeño; cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add the tomato paste, smoked paprika, cumin, and oregano; cook for 2 minutes, stirring to combine.
Pour in the vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, making sure the temperature stays between 80–85°C (190–185°F).
Add the paneer cubes and lime juice; simmer uncovered for 15 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the paneer to absorb the broth.
Season with salt and pepper to taste; adjust lime juice for brightness if desired.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh cilantro if using, and enjoy a light, bitter Mexican paneer stew.
